file_insights.purpose: Integration test suite for complete order flow
key_components: Order lifecycle tests (create → confirm → prepare → ready → deliver → complete)
Mock factories for Order, OrderItem, Table, OrderRepository, TableRepository, EventService
test_coverage: Complete order flow for dine-in orders
Multiple orders with unique order numbers

patterns_discovered.description: Utility functions (createMockOrderRepository, createMockTableRepository, createMockEventService, createMockOrder, etc.) for consistent test data generation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces test boilerplate and ensures consistent mock structure across tests

patterns_discovered.description: Tests verify interactions between OrderService, CartService, TableRepository, and EventService through their dependencies
patterns_discovered.benefit: Validates complete workflows rather than isolated units

patterns_discovered.description: Integration tests verify that appropriate events are emitted during order state transitions (order.created, order.status_changed)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures event-driven architecture compliance and async operation tracking

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive testing of order status transitions: PENDING → CONFIRMED → PREPARING → READY → DELIVERED → COMPLETED
patterns_discovered.benefit: Validates complex state machine behavior and prevents invalid state transitions

patterns_discovered.description: Tests verify that orders and tables are properly scoped to organizationId to prevent cross-tenant data leakage
patterns_discovered.benefit: Critical for multi-tenant SaaS applications to maintain data isolation

patterns_discovered.description: Tests verify relationships between orders, tables, and items are maintained correctly throughout lifecycle
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents orphaned records and ensures database consistency

gotchas_discovered.description: Dine-in orders must be linked to tables via setCurrentOrder, but takeaway orders should not be
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incorrect implementation could link tables to non-dine-in orders, causing business logic errors
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Test explicitly verifies table linking is only called for dine-in orders

gotchas_discovered.description: When an order is completed, the table's currentOrderId must be set to null to make it available for new orders
gotchas_discovered.impact: Missing cleanup could mark tables as permanently occupied
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Integration test verifies setCurrentOrder is called with null on completion

gotchas_discovered.description: Orders must transition through valid status sequences; skipping states or invalid transitions should fail
gotchas_discovered.impact: Invalid state transitions could create inconsistent order data
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests validate specific status transitions and previousStatus values

gotchas_discovered.description: Events must be emitted at correct lifecycle points, and multiple events can be emitted in sequence
gotchas_discovered.impact: Missing or out-of-order events could break downstream systems relying on event-driven architecture
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests capture and verify all emitted events throughout the flow

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple simultaneous order creations or status updates could cause race conditions or duplicate order numbers
gotchas_discovered.impact: Business logic errors, duplicate orders, or lost updates
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Test includes concurrent operation scenarios to verify thread-safety

gotchas_discovered.description: All repository operations must be scoped to organizationId to prevent cross-tenant data access
gotchas_discovered.impact: Critical security vulnerability if not properly scoped
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests verify orgId is passed to all service methods and repository operations

approach_outcome.description: Comprehensive integration test suite successfully created for order flow
implementation_approach: Created mock factory functions for consistent test data generation
Structured tests into logical describe blocks: complete flow, CRUD operations, status transitions, item management, isolation, and error handling
Used beforeEach hook to reset mocks for each test, ensuring test isolation
Verified service interactions with repositories and event service
Tested both happy path and edge cases (concurrent operations, invalid transitions, etc.)
Ensured proper event emission at each stage
approach_outcome.test_count: 50+ test cases covering all major order flow scenarios
key_achievements: Event emission verification for event-driven architecture

recommendations.recommendation: Consider extracting mock factories into a separate test utility file (e.g., tests/integration/factories.ts) to reduce file size and improve reusability across other test suites
Add parameterized tests for different order types (DINE_IN, TAKEAWAY, DELIVERY) to ensure consistent behavior across all order types
Add more descriptive assertion messages using jest/vitest expect().toEqual(x, 'description') syntax to improve debugging when tests fail
Add performance tests to verify order operations don't exceed SLA response times, especially for concurrent scenarios
Expand error handling tests to cover repository failures, concurrent update conflicts, and eventual consistency scenarios
Add tests validating invariants at each state (e.g., COMPLETED orders must have all timestamps set, PENDING orders must have no delivery date)
Add tests validating integration with CartService to ensure cart items properly convert to order items with correct calculations
